title: Contract developer vs agency | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)

seo.title: Contract developer vs agency | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
seo.description: A practical comparison of senior contract development support and agency delivery for companies working on custom software, takeovers, or internal systems.

page.title: Contract developer vs agency | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: Contract developer vs agency: which model fits the project
page.description: A practical comparison of senior contract development support and agency delivery for companies working on custom software, takeovers, or internal systems.

hero.title: The right choice depends on responsibility, not vendor size
hero.subtitle: Sometimes direct senior involvement is the stronger option. In other cases broader agency capacity is the better fit.

intro: The difference is not just cost. The bigger question is whether the project needs direct senior ownership or a wider supplier setup with multiple roles.
Inherited apps and complex product situations often benefit from direct technical ownership rather than heavier delivery theatre.

sections.title: When a contract developer is the stronger fit
body: This works best when the company needs a senior person to take ownership of a defined technical area, plug into an existing team quickly, or stabilise a sensitive project situation.

sections.title: When an agency makes more sense
body: An agency can be the better fit when the project truly needs a broader set of roles at once, a larger supplier framework, or a fuller outsourcing model.

sections.title: How to decide
body: The decision should not be based only on price or technology taste. What matters is operational fit, change speed, and long-term cost.
bullets: sensitivity of the takeover or delivery context

sections.title: Practical conclusion
body: If the project needs senior technical ownership and close integration with an existing team, a contract developer is often the stronger model. If the company needs wider supplier coverage, an agency may be the better option.

faq.question: Is an agency automatically safer?
faq.answer: Not always. Safety depends on capability, ownership, and fit. In difficult application work, direct senior accountability can be more valuable than team size alone.

faq.question: Can a contract developer deliver a whole workstream?
faq.answer: Yes. For a well-defined technical area or a smaller project slice, that is often the right setup.

faq.question: What if we later need broader capacity?
faq.answer: The delivery model can evolve. The key is choosing the model that fits the current project reality rather than future hypotheticals.

faq.question: Is contract work only about coding?
faq.answer: No. In senior engagements it often includes architecture, prioritisation, takeover work, and delivery simplification.

for: sensitivity of the takeover or delivery context
notFor: abstract technology debates with no business context
